assertion_results.text: Documentation includes an Authentication section explaining JWT Bearer token format
assertion_results.evidence: Authentication section present at bottom of doc with JWT Bearer token format and header example

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint clearly indicates whether authentication is required or optional
assertion_results.evidence: POST/PUT/DELETE say 'Requires authentication' but GET endpoints don't explicitly state they are public. No consistent per-endpoint auth indicator.

assertion_results.text: Rate limiting is documented with specific limits (100/min authenticated, 20/min anonymous)
assertion_results.evidence: No rate limiting documentation found anywhere in the output

assertion_results.text: Paginated endpoints document pagination parameters (page, per_page or cursor) with defaults
assertion_results.evidence: GET /api/posts mentions 'page and per_page parameters' but no defaults, types, or constraints. Comments endpoint just says 'Supports pagination'.

assertion_results.text: Response schema for list endpoints shows the pagination envelope (items, total, has_more)
assertion_results.evidence: No response examples or schemas shown for any endpoint. No pagination envelope documented.

assertion_results.text: Public read endpoints (GET) are distinguished from authenticated write endpoints (POST, PUT, DELETE)
assertion_results.evidence: Auth section says 'Some endpoints are public (GET requests)' but individual GET endpoints don't state this. Distinction is vague.

assertion_results.text: At least one curl example includes the Authorization header with a Bearer token
assertion_results.evidence: No curl examples in the documentation

assertion_results.text: The OpenAPI spec includes a securitySchemes component defining the JWT bearer scheme
assertion_results.evidence: No OpenAPI JSON spec was produced
